title: Clean Core readiness in Teams
subtitle: Teams replay showing custom-code evidence, release state, and a modernization summary.
outcome: A risk card with evidence and released successor suggestions.

read.summary: ARC-1 reads the relevant custom-code evidence.
read.result:
SELECT * FROM usr02 ...
SELECT * FROM but000 ...

panel.title: Custom-code evidence
panel.code:
SELECT SINGLE * FROM usr02 INTO @DATA(user_row) WHERE bname = @iv_user.
SELECT SINGLE * FROM but000 INTO @DATA(bp_row) WHERE partner = @iv_partner.

api-state.summary: ARC-1 asks the SAP system for release-state evidence.
api-state.result: USR02: internal table, no released contract. BUT000: use released business partner API/view.

items.label: USR02
items.value: Internal table; use I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC where applicable

atc.summary: ARC-1 adds SAP-side quality signals to the source evidence.

options.label: Backlog item
options.description: Turn the evidence into a modernization task.

panel.title: Clean Core risk card
panel.body: Not clean-core ready. The class directly reads internal SAP tables and should be refactored toward released APIs or released CDS views before a cloud-readiness target.

backlog.text:
## Backlog item

**Refactor `ZCL_ARC1_DEMO_CCORE` away from direct `USR02` / `BUT000` reads.**

Acceptance criteria:

- No direct internal SAP table reads.
- Replacement uses a released API or view where available (e.g. `I_BusinessUserBasic`, released business partner views).
- ATC stays clean.
- Behavior is covered by unit tests.

done.text: Useful in Teams because the answer is **evidence-backed** — not a generic Clean Core explanation.
